αGoalOSProof Horizon Console
Hub Command Loop → RSI → ASI RSI Room Evidence Boundary Institution

Public proof demonstration · Loop → RSI → ASI horizon

Capability rises.
Authority narrows.

A browser-local console for the core GoalOS idea: write the loop, prove the loop, govern recursive improvement, and stop at the human boundary before any future superintelligence-grade authority could touch the world.

Open the console

No account. No form. No wallet. No token approval. No network request. No analytics. No cookies. No browser storage. No transaction broadcast. No production authority. No user data wanted.

Horizon stateREADY

Proof

E0evidence
DENYauthority
0%docket
Loop lawstate survives context
RSI lawsearch is not authority

GoalOS lawonly proof enters memory
01 · objective contract

Choose the mandate, then run the proof corridor.

02 · proof horizon corridor
Loopprocedure over promptcontract, progress, trace, restart
RSIimprovement under gatesECI, baselines, replay, stress
ASI horizonhigher capability, narrower authoritycontainment, council, rollback, human review

Ready. The console starts with no authority: a loop can generate output, but only proof gates decide whether anything can become reusable institutional memory.

03 · authority firewall

No evidence, no promotion.

Contract completePENDING
Replay integrityPENDING
Evidence contactE0
Baseline advantagePENDING
Shock persistencePENDING
Containment bandPASS
Architect / Validator CouncilREQUIRED
Human boundaryREQUIRED
OutcomeAuthority = RiskGate × EvidenceGate × BaselineGate × ReplayGate × PersistenceGate × CouncilGate × HumanBoundary.
Search may allocate attention. It never grants authority.
04 · virtual evidence disk

      

      

        
05 · Chronicle trace

        
    
        
    
      
    
    
    

    
    
      
    Loop
    The loop is memory with a stop rule.
    It writes the contract, trace, files, evaluator rubric, restart point, and bottleneck outside the short context window.
    
      
    RSI
    Recursive improvement is a governance problem.
    Candidate improvements can be explored, but they cannot insert, settle, or promote until evidence, replay, baselines, and safety gates pass.
    
      
    ASI horizon
    Stronger systems require narrower permissions.
    Future superintelligence-grade capability would require stricter containment, independent councils, rollback, challenge windows, and explicit human or institutional authority.
    
    
    

    
    
      
    
        
    Claim boundary
    
        
    ASI is handled here as a proof horizon, not as an achieved capability.
    
        
    This page does not claim achieved AGI, achieved ASI, superintelligence, empirical SOTA, external audit completion, production certification, autonomous sovereignty, live invention authority, or civilization-scale capability. Stronger claims require real tasks, baseline comparisons, executed evidence, replayable ProofBundles, safety/cost ledgers, validator reports, delayed outcomes, and independent review.
